facts: The patient's name is Mark Jackson.
The patient is a 45-year-old male.
The patient has a history of diabetes type two.
The patient has a history of mild intermittent asthma.
The patient is experiencing right elbow pain.
The patient has been playing a lot of tennis recently.
The patient's elbow pain started after increasing tennis activity.
The patient's elbow pain has lasted for three weeks.
The pain is located on the outside part of the elbow.
The patient denied pain radiating to other areas.
The patient rates the pain as an 8 out of 10 when using the elbow.
The patient rates the pain as a 3 out of 10 when resting.
The patient describes the pain as sharp, stabbing, and aching.
The patient has not had any recent trauma to the elbow.
The patient has tried Tylenol and ibuprofen for pain relief.
The patient has tried icing the elbow for pain relief.
The patient used to be a big tennis player.
The patient was a state champ in high school.
The patient's blood sugars have been running around 150-160.
The patient eats candy, including Cadbury eggs.
The patient is taking metformin.
The metformin dose is 500 milligrams.
Metformin is taken twice a day.
Metformin is prescribed for diabetes.
The patient is taking Flovent.
The Flovent dose is not specified.
Flovent is taken twice a day.
Flovent is prescribed for asthma.
The patient is taking albuterol.
The albuterol dose is not specified.
Albuterol is taken as needed.
Albuterol is prescribed for asthma.
The patient has an allergy to penicillin.
The patient had their gallbladder removed.
The patient had their gallbladder removed by Doctor Nelson.
The patient had their gallbladder removed about 10 years ago.
The doctor examined the patient.
The patient's heart exam showed a grade 3 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur.
The patient's cardiovascular exam was otherwise normal.
The patient's musculoskeletal exam showed moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow.
The patient has moderate pain with passive range of motion of the right elbow.
The patient has mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist.
The patient's right elbow x-ray showed no acute fracture or bony abnormality.
The patient's right elbow x-ray was otherwise normal.
The doctor diagnosed the patient with acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow.
The doctor prescribed rest for the patient's right arm.
The doctor advised the patient to stop playing tennis for 4-6 weeks.
The doctor advised the patient to avoid repetitive stress on the right arm.
The doctor advised the patient to ice the right elbow for 20 minutes, three times a day.
The doctor prescribed a brace for the patient's right forearm.
The doctor prescribed Ultram.
The Ultram dose is 50 milligrams.
Ultram is taken once per day.
Ultram is prescribed for elbow pain.
The doctor scheduled a follow-up appointment in 4 weeks.
The doctor ordered a hemoglobin A1c level.
The doctor ordered a CBC blood test.
The doctor ordered a chem-12 blood test.
The patient's diabetes treatment plan was adjusted.
The patient's metformin dose was increased to 1000 milligrams twice a day.
The doctor advised the patient to check their blood sugar twice daily for 2 weeks.
The patient's asthma treatment plan was not changed.
The doctor refilled the patient's albuterol prescription.
The patient is to use the Flovent and albuterol as tolerated.
